# 727a8.com

> Free guide to the Chapter 7 discharge bar under 11 U.S.C. Section 727(a)(8).

727a8.com is a free, open-source educational resource about the 8-year Chapter 7 discharge waiting period and all related bankruptcy discharge timing bars. Part of the Bankruptcy Transparency Network.

## Pages

- https://727a8.com/: Complete guide to Section 727(a)(8) -- the 8-year Chapter 7 discharge bar. Filing date to filing date. No exceptions. The longest bar in the Bankruptcy Code.
- https://727a8.com/calculator.html: Free calculator -- enter your prior filing date, instantly see when you can file Chapter 7 again. Handles 727(a)(8) and 727(a)(9).
- https://727a8.com/exceptions.html: Section 727(a)(9) -- the 6-year exception. Chapter 7 after Chapter 13 with two exceptions for 100% and 70%+ repayment plans.
- https://727a8.com/filing-to-filing.html: Filing date to filing date -- how the bar is measured. Common misconception that it runs from discharge date. Case law: In re Blendheim.
- https://727a8.com/chapter-13-after-7.html: Filing Chapter 13 after Chapter 7. Section 1328(f)(1) 4-year bar. The Chapter 20 strategy explained.
- https://727a8.com/all-bars-compared.html: All bankruptcy discharge bars compared -- 727(a)(8), 727(a)(9), 1328(f)(1), 1328(f)(2), 109(g). Complete table with visual timeline.
- https://727a8.com/727a9.html: Section 727(a)(9) -- the 6-year bar after a Chapter 13 discharge
- https://727a8.com/case-law.html: Key case law interpreting Section 727(a)(8) and 727(a)(9)
- https://727a8.com/vs-1328f.html: 727(a)(8) vs 1328(f) -- comparing Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 discharge bars

## Key Facts

- Section 727(a)(8) bars a Chapter 7 discharge for 8 years after a prior Chapter 7 or Chapter 11 discharge
- Section 727(a)(9) bars a Chapter 7 discharge for 6 years after a prior Chapter 13 or Chapter 12 discharge, with exceptions for 100% and 70%+ plans
- All discharge bars are measured filing date to filing date, not from the discharge date
- These are discharge bars, not filing bars -- you can file at any time, but the discharge will be denied
- There is no discharge bar for Chapter 11 or Chapter 12 following any prior discharge
- Section 109(g) is a separate filing bar (180 days) that applies after certain dismissals

## Network

Part of the Bankruptcy Transparency Network:
- 1328f.com -- Discharge eligibility screener
- 1328f.org -- Research platform
- 109g.org -- The 180-day filing bar
- dischargebar.org -- All discharge timing bars
- section1328.org -- Chapter 13 discharge rules
- automaticstay.org -- The automatic stay
- meanstest.org -- Chapter 7 means test
- 341meeting.org -- Meeting of creditors
- 523a.org -- Nondischargeable debts
- relieffromstay.org -- Relief from the automatic stay
- dischargeinjunction.com -- The discharge injunction
- prosedebtors.org -- Resources for pro se debtors

## Contact

This site is maintained by volunteers. No legal advice is provided.
